questions.question: Which Spring Boot test annotation boots only the MVC layer (controllers, JSON, validation) and not JPA or other infrastructure?
questions.explanation: @WebMvcTest loads a slice of the application context: MVC, controllers, MockMvc, validation, message converters. It does not load JPA or service beans by default - use @MockBean to provide collaborators. @SpringBootTest boots the full context (slower).

questions.question: What's the Mockito equivalent of Moq's mock.Setup(x => x.Save(It.IsAny<Order>())).Returns(saved)?
questions.explanation: Mockito uses when(...).thenReturn(...) for stubbing and verify(...) for verification. any() is the wildcard matcher. The BDD-style alternative is given(...).willReturn(...) from BDDMockito, but the when/thenReturn form is standard.

questions.question: You want to integration-test a JPA repository against real Postgres (not H2). What's the standard approach in 2026?
choices: Manually start Postgres before running tests and configure spring.datasource.url.
Use Testcontainers with @ServiceConnection on a PostgreSQLContainer - Spring Boot auto-wires the connection. Run tests with Docker available.
Switch to JdbcTemplate; JPA can't be tested against real Postgres.
Use @SpringBootTest(properties = "spring.datasource.url=jdbc:postgresql://localhost") with a pre-installed local server.
questions.explanation: Testcontainers boots a Postgres container per test class (or once per JVM with the static + @Container pattern) and Spring Boot's @ServiceConnection (3.1+) wires it into the data source automatically. No manual property registration needed.
